name: booktest

metadata.name: booktest
metadata.summary: Booktest is a snapshot testing library for review driven testing.

documentation:
# Booktest

booktest is review-driven testing tool that combines Jupyterbook style data science
development with traditional regression testing. Booktest is developed by 
[Lumoa.me](https://lumoa.me), the actionable feedback analytics platform.

booktest is designed to tackle a common problem with the data science
RnD work flows and regression testing: 

 * Data science produces results such as probability estimates, which can be 
   good or bad, but not really right or wrong as in the traditional software engineering. 
     * Because the DS results are not strictly right or wrong, it's very difficult to use assertions 
       for quality assurance and preventing regression.
     * For example, you cannot really say that accuracy 0.84 is correct, while the 
       accuracy 0.83 is incorrect, especially if you have other measurements (log likelihood)
       giving conflicting results. Neither evaluating a topic model as correct or incorrect
       is non-sensical. In practice, most data science applications require an expert 
       review.
     * This less ambigious quality also creates need for a better visibility of how
       the system behaves. One typically wants to print out edge cases and their diagnostics
       to see the behavior, see intermediate steps and see the results for different data sets . 
 * There is also the problem of the data science data being big and the intermediate 
   results being computationally expensive. 
     * Jupyter notebook deals with this problem by keeping the state in memory between runs, while 
       traditional unittests tend to lose the program state between runs. This leads to very slow 
       test runs, slow iteration speed and low productivity.
 * While the Jupyter Notebook provides good visibility to results required by the expert review and
   powerful caching functionality: it fails short on a) often requiring copy-pasting production code to 
   make results visible, b) it doesn't support automated regression testing and c) expert review requires
   expensive full review even if nothing changed.

booktest solves this problem setting by delivering on 3 main points:

 * Focus on the results and analytic as in Jupyter notebook by allowing user to print
   the results as MD files. 
 * Keep the intermediate results cached either in memory or in filesystem by
   having two level cache.
 * Instead of doing strict assertions, do testing by comparing old results with 
   new results.

As such, booktest does snapshot testing, and it stores the snapshots in filesystem and in Git. 
Additional benefit of this approach is that you can trace the result development in Git.

# Getting started guide

You can find getting started guide [here](getting-started.md)

# Workflows, coverage and CI

You can find guide on common workflows, coverage measurements and 
continuous integration [here](workflows.md)

# Examples

Examples are found in the [test example directory](test/examples). 

Example results are visible in the [book index](books/index.md).

There is also [separate example project](https://github.com/lumoa-oss/booktest-example)


# API reference

API reference is generated under [docs](docs) directory. Main classes are:

 * [TestCaseRun](docs/testcaserun.py.md), which provides API for tests
 * [TestBook](docs/testbook.py.md), which provide a base class for test suite object
 * [TestSuite](docs/testsuite.py.md), which provide a base class for test suite object
 * [Tests](docs/tests.py.md), which manages CLI interface

# Developing booktest

Development guide is available [here](development.md)

modules.name: booktest.tokenizer
documentation: Text processing used in test

classes.name: booktest.tokenizer.TestTokenizer
documentation: simple tokenizer, that tokenizes whitespaces, words and numbers

functions.name: booktest.reports.write_lines
documentation: IO helper utilities

classes.name: booktest.reports.TestResult
documentation: Test results and CLI user interaction

classes.name: booktest.reports.Metrics
documentation: Stores the top level test metrics/results
Saved test reporting

classes.name: booktest.reports.CaseReports
documentation: This class manages the saved case specific metrics/results

classes.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.report
documentation: writes a report line in report log and possibly in standard output

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.reset_exp_reader
documentation: Resets the reader that reads expectation / snapshot file

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.tmp_path
documentation:
creates a temporary file with the filename in the test's .tmp directory

these files get deleted before new runs, and by `booktest --clean` command

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.file
documentation:
creates a file with the filename in the test's main directory

these files can include test output or e.g. images and graphs included in the
.md output. NOTE: these files may end up in Git, so keep them small
and avoid sensitive information.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.rename_file_to_hash
documentation:
this can be useful with images or similar resources it avoids overwrites
(e.g. image.png won't be renamed with image.pnh), guarantees uniqueness
and makes the test break whenever image changes.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.rel_path
documentation:
rel_path returns relative path for a file. the returned path that can be referred
from the MD file e.g. in images

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.start
documentation: Internal method: starts the test run with the given title

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.review
documentation:
Internal method: runs the review step, which is done at the end of the test.
This method is typically called by end method()

This step may be interactive depending on the configuration. It ends up with
the user or automation accepting or rejecting the result.

Returns test result (TEST, DIFF, OK) and interaction value, which is used to signal e.g.
test run termination.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.end
documentation:
Test ending step. This records the test time, closes resources,
        and sets up preliminary result (FAIL, DIFF, OK). This also
        reports the case and calls the review step.
s
        :return:

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.close_exp_reader
documentation:
Closes the expectation/snapshot file reader
:return:

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.close
documentation: Closes all resources (e.g. file system handles).

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.next_exp_line
documentation: Moves snapshot reader cursor to the next snapshot file line

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.jump
documentation:
Moves the snapshot reader cursor to the specified line number.

If line number is before current reader position, the snapshot
file reader is reset.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.seek
documentation:
Seeks the next snapshot/expectation file line that matches the
is_line_ok() lambda. The seeking is started on 'begin' line and
it ends on the 'end' line.

NOTE: The seeks starts from the cursor position,
but it may restart seeking from the beginning of the file,
if the sought line is not found.

NOTE: this is really an O(N) scanning operation.
      it may restart at the beginning of file and
      it typically reads the the entire file
      on seek failures.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.seek_line
documentation:
Seeks the next snapshot/expectation file line matching the anchor.

NOTE: The seeks starts from the cursor position,
but it may restart seeking from the beginning of the file,
if the sought line is not found.

NOTE: this is really an O(N) scanning operation.
      it may restart at the beginning of file and
      it typically reads the the entire file
      on seek failures.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.seek_prefix
documentation:
Seeks the next snapshot/expectation file line matching the prefix.

NOTE: The seeks starts from the cursor position,
but it may restart seeking from the beginning of the file,
if the sought line is not found.

NOTE: this is really an O(N) scanning operation.
      it may restart at the beginning of file and
      it typically reads the the entire file
      on seek failures.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.write_line
documentation:
Internal method. Writes a line into test output file and moves
the snaphost line forward by one.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.commit_line
documentation:
Internal method. Commits the prepared line into testing.

This writes both decorated line into reporting AND this writes
the line into test output. Also the snapshot file cursor is
moved into next line.

Statistics line number of differing or erroneous lines get
updated.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.head_exp_token
documentation: Returns the next token in the snapshot file without moving snapshot file cursor

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.next_exp_token
documentation:
Reads the next token from the snapshot file. NOTE: this moves snapshot file
cursor into the next token.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.feed_token
documentation:
Feeds a token into test stream. If `check` is True, the token
will be compared to the next awaiting token in the snapshot file,
and on difference a 'diff' is reported.

If `check`is True, snapshot file cursor is also moved, but no
comparison is made.

NOTE: if token is a line end character, the line will be committed
to the test stream.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.test_feed_token
documentation:
Feeds a token into test stream. The token will be compared to the next
awaiting token in the snapshot file, and on difference a 'diff' is reported.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.test_feed
documentation:
Feeds a piece text into the test stream. The text tokenized and feed
into text stream as individual tokens.

NOTE: The token content IS COMPARED to snapshot content for differences
that are reported.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.feed
documentation:
Feeds a piece text into the test stream. The text tokenized and feed
into text stream as individual tokens.

NOTE: The token content IS NOT COMPARED to snapshot content, and differences
are ignored

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.diff
documentation: an unexpected difference encountered. this method marks a difference on the line manually

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.fail
documentation: a proper failure encountered. this method marks an error on the line manually

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.anchor
documentation:
creates a prefix anchor by seeking & printing prefix. e.g. if you have "key=" anchor,
the snapshot cursor will be moved to next line starting with "key=" prefix.

This method is used for controlling the snapshot cursor location and guaranteeing
that a section in test is compared against correct section in the snapshot

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.anchorln
documentation:
creates a line anchor by seeking & printing an anchor line. e.g. if you have "# SECTION 3" anchor,
the snapshot cursor will be moved to next "# SECTION 3" line.

This method is used for controlling the snapshot cursor location and guaranteeing
that a section in test is compared against correct section in the snapshot

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.header
documentation:
creates a header line that also operates as an anchor.

the only difference between this method and anchorln() method is that the
header is preceded and followed by an empty line.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.tmsln
documentation:
runs the function f and measures the time milliseconds it took.
the measurement is printed in the test stream and compared into previous
result in the snaphost file.

This method also prints a new line after the measurements.

NOTE: if max_ms is defined, this line will fail, if the test took more than
max_ms milliseconds.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.imsln
documentation:
runs the function f and measures the time milliseconds it took.
the measurement is printed in the test stream and compared into previous
result in the snaphost file.

This method also prints a new line after the measurements.

NOTE: unline tmsln(), this method never fails or marks a difference.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.h
documentation: Markdown style header at level specified by `level` parameter

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.h1
documentation:
Markdown style header 1st level header

This method is used to mark titles as in

```python
t.h1("This is my title")
t.tln("This is my title")
```

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.h2
documentation: Markdown style header 2nd level header

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.h3
documentation: Markdown style header 3rd level header

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.timage
documentation: Adds a markdown image in the test stream with specified alt text

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.ttable
documentation:
Writes a markdown table based on the `table` parameter columns. It uses column
keys as column names

```python
t.ttable({
  "x": [1, 2, 3],
  "y": [2, 3, 4]
})
```

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.tdf
documentation:
Writes the `df` dataframe as a markdown table.

NOTE: df should be of pd.DataFrame or compatible type

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.tlist
documentation:
Writes the list into test stream. By default, the list
is prefixed by markdown ' * ' list expression.

For example following call:

```python
t.tlist(["a", "b", "c"])
```

will produce:

 * a
 * b
 * c

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.tset
documentation:
This method used to print and compare a set of items to expected set
in out of order fashion. It will first scan the next elements
based on prefix. After this step, it will check whether the items
were in the list.

NOTE: this method may be slow, if the set order is unstable.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.assertln
documentation:
Fails the line if the assertion is false.

This is typically used in unit testing style assertions like:

```python
t.t("is HTTP response code 200? ").assertln(response.code() == 200)
```

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.must_apply
documentation:
Assertions with decoration for testing, whether `it`
fulfills a condition.

Maily used by TestIt class

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.must_contain
documentation:
Assertions with decoration for testing, whether `it`
contains a member.

Maily used by TestIt class

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.must_equal
documentation:
Assertions with decoration for testing, whether `it`
equals something.

Maily used by TestIt class

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.must_be_a
documentation:
Assertions with decoration for testing, whether `it`
is of specific type.

Maily used by TestIt class

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.it
documentation:
Creates TestIt class around the `it` object named with `name`

This can be used for assertions as in:

```python
result = [1, 2]
t.it("result", result).must_be_a(list).must_contain(1).must_contain(2)
```

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.t
documentation: Writes the text into test stream. NOTE: this will not print a newline.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.tformat
documentation:
Converts the value into json like structure containing only the value types.

Prints a json containing the value types.

Mainly used for getting snapshot of a e.g. Json response format.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.tln
documentation: Prints a newline to tests stream. NOTE: this will commit and check the buffered test line

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.keyvalueln
documentation:
Prints a value of format "{key} {value}", and uses key as prefix anchor for
adjusting the snapshot file cursor.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.tln
documentation: Writes the text and new line into test stream. This will commit the test line.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.i
documentation:
Writes the text into test stream without testing the text against snapshot.

'i' comes from 'info'/'ignore'.

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestCaseRun.iln
documentation:
Writes the text and new line into test stream without testing the text against the snapshot.

'i' comes from 'info'/'ignore'.

A utility, that manages an invidiual test run, and provides the
main API for the test case

classes.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestIt

methods.name: booktest.testcaserun.TestIt.member
documentation: Creates a TestIt class for the member of 'it'
utility for making assertions related to a specific object

Generates a resource for given port.
A special identifier is generated in order to not mix the port

functions.name: booktest.dependencies.depends_on

classes.name: booktest.dependencies.Resource
documentation:
Represents an exclusive resources, which must not be
shared simultaneously by several parallel tests

Such a resource can be a specific port, file system resource,
some global state or excessive use of RAM or GPU, that prohibits parallel
run.

functions.name: booktest.naming.camel_case_to_snake_case
documentation: Utilities related to test naming

functions.name: booktest.testsuite.drop_prefix
documentation:
removes a prefix like 'test' from all test.
this can be used, if the test name inference adds
unnecessary decorations to test names.
Test suite manipulation (renames, merges)

functions.name: booktest.testsuite.merge_tests
documentation: Combines a list of Tests into a single Tests entity

methods.name: booktest.cache.LruCache.__init__
documentation: This can be provided as the memory cache to avoid
running out of memory during the test

methods.name: booktest.cache.NoCache.__len__
documentation: This can be provided to skip using in-memory cache
as in-memory cache may lead to memory usage issues.

modules.name: booktest.cli
documentation:
This package introduces the lumoa-rl cli interface.
It can be used for creating insights or for creating topics.

modules.name: booktest.booktest
documentation:
Book test addresses problems with data science RnD workflow and regression
testing.

In data science:

 1. There are often no right/wrong answers, but good/bad answers.
    This emphasizes a need to see and compare changes in results
 2. the data is big and state preparations are costly
 3. At the same time, there is a need for regression testing

For this reason, there is a need for a tool, that handles ability to
see results and the cacheing as nicely as the Jupyter notebook, but that also
supports regression testing similarly to traditional unit test suites.

methods.name: booktest.runs.RunBatch.__init__
documentation: Tests are collected into suites, that are
treated as test batches run by process pools

methods.name: booktest.testbook.TestBook.__init__
documentation:
if path is None, a default path is generated. If name is not None,
the name is used as a part of the default path name.

The full_path parameter is aimed for e.g. generating different test
cases from the same class, while name is aimed for fixing badly
generated names (e.g. caused by all caps abbreviations).

Set a mocked method onto the target.

If method is a bound_method, can direct setattr

methods.name: booktest.requests.SnapshotRequests.stop

Stop mocking requests.

This should have no impact if mocking has not been started.
When nesting mockers, make sure to stop the innermost first.

methods.name: booktest.tests.Tests.exec_parsed
documentation:
---
param cache: |-
  in-memory cache. Can be e.g. dictionary {},
  LruCache or NoCache.
param parsed: the object containing argparse parsed arguments
param root_dir: the directory containing books and .out directory
return: returns an exit value. 0 for success, 1 for error
---

methods.name: booktest.tests.Tests.exec
documentation:
---
param args: a string containing command line arguments
param cache: |-
  in-memory cache. Can be e.g. dictionary {},
  LruCache or NoCache.
param root_dir: the directory containing books and .out directory
return: returns an exit value. 0 for success, 1 for error
---
